title: OpenIM SDK for uni-app
description: OpenIM uni-app SDK entry point for App, H5, and supported mini-app targets.
keywords: sdk uniapp v4 chat sdk v4 uniapp overview
content: Overview Use the uni app SDK when the same OpenIM integration needs to cover App, H5, and supported mini app targets from a uni app codebase. The integration should keep authentication, user identity, message creation, conversation state, and event handling aligned with the rest of the OpenIM SDK family. Platform scope App and H5 builds should use the SDK package and runtime adapter recommended by the OpenIM release you deploy. Mini app targets need additional validation for storage, network, file upload, and websocket behavior. Tokens should still be issued by a trusted backend. Do not generate or hard code user tokens in the client bundle. Core integration path 1. Install the SDK package that matches your OpenIM Server version. 2. Initialize the client with apiAddr , wsAddr , the current userID , and a backend issued token. 3. Register connection and message events before calling login . 4. Send the first text message, then validate message receipt in another signed in client. 5. Add platform specific handling for file messages, push notifications, and background lifecycle. Related SDKs WASM SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview for browser and WebAssembly oriented API examples. Flutter SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/overview when mobile and desktop should be handled through Flutter. React Native SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/overview when the app is built with React Native.

title: Freeze and unfreeze a channel
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for Freeze and unfreeze a channel, with API signatures, usage details, and verification steps.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm channel moderating-a-channel freeze-and-unfreeze-a-channel
content: Overview Mute or unmute an OpenIM group to freeze channel wide message sending, or mute a member for a fixed duration. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model Sendbird freeze maps most closely to OpenIM group mute. Member level mute is separate and uses seconds. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Have a valid groupID and a current user with the required group role. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; changeGroupMute: data: ChangeGroupMuteParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; changeGroupMemberMute: data: ChangeGroupMemberMuteParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getGroupMemberList: params: GetGroupMemberParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnGroupInfoChanged CbEvents.OnGroupMemberInfoChanged Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Mute the whole group or one member. await OpenIM.changeGroupMute groupID, isMute: true ; await OpenIM.changeGroupMemberMute groupID, userID, mutedSeconds: 3600, ; Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnGroupInfoChanged , CbEvents.OnGroupMemberInfoChanged . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

title: Overview
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for Overview, with API signatures, usage details, and verification steps.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm channel overview-channel
content: Overview Use the channel section to map Sendbird channel concepts to OpenIM conversations and groups: create groups, join or invite members, retrieve channel lists, search groups, and moderate group state. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model A Sendbird group channel usually maps to an OpenIM group plus its conversation. Direct chats are conversations addressed by recvID . OpenIM does not expose a Sendbird open channel object in the WASM SDK. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; createGroup: data: CreateGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; joinGroup: data: JoinGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; inviteUserToGroup: data: AccessToGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getJoinedGroupList: operationID?: string = Promise ; getSpecifiedGroupsInfo: params: string , operationID?: string = Promise ; getAllConversationList: operationID?: string = Promise ; getOneConversation: params: GetOneConversationParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; searchGroups: data: SearchGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; dismissGroup: data: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnNewConversation CbEvents.OnConversationChanged CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupAdded CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupDeleted CbEvents.OnGroupInfoChanged CbEvents.OnGroupDismissed Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Load conversations for the visible channel list and groups for group metadata. const data: conversations = await OpenIM.getAllConversationList ; const data: groups = await OpenIM.getJoinedGroupList ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnConversationChanged, data = mergeConversations data ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnGroupInfoChanged, data = mergeGroups data ; Create a group for member based group chat. Use direct messages for one to one conversations instead of creating a channel first. Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnNewConversation , CbEvents.OnConversationChanged , CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupAdded , CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupDeleted , CbEvents.OnGroupInfoChanged , CbEvents.OnGroupDismissed . Always log operationID with failures. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm refresh and reconnect behavior matches the product requirement. title: Send your first message

content: Overview Connect a browser client, listen for incoming messages, create a text MessageItem , and send it to a user or group. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model A direct message sets recvID and leaves groupID empty. A group message sets groupID and leaves recvID empty. OpenIM creates the message object first, then sends it. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Have a target conversationID , recvID , or groupID depending on whether the flow is direct or group chat. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; login: params: InitAndLoginConfig, operationID?: string = Promise ; createTextMessage: text: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnConnecting CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess CbEvents.OnConnectFailed CbEvents.OnUserTokenExpired CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Create and reuse one SDK instance in the browser session. import CbEvents, getSDK from '@openim/wasm client sdk'; const OpenIM = getSDK coreWasmPath: '/openIM.wasm', sqlWasmPath: '/sql wasm.wasm', ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess, = console.info 'OpenIM connected' ; ; await OpenIM.login userID, token, platformID: 5, apiAddr, wsAddr, ; Create the message object first, then send it. const data: message = await OpenIM.createTextMessage 'hello openim' ; const data: sentMessage = await OpenIM.sendMessage recvID: receiverUserID, groupID: '', message, ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages, data = for const item of data renderIncomingMessage item ; ; For a group message, pass recvID: '' and the target groupID . Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnConnecting , CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess , CbEvents.OnConnectFailed , CbEvents.OnUserTokenExpired , CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. title: Create a scheduled message
description: Support status, OpenIM alternatives, and WASM SDK notes for Create a scheduled message.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm message managing-scheduled-messages-in-group-channel create-a-scheduled-message
content: Overview OpenIM WASM SDK has no public scheduled message create, update, cancel, list, or send now API. This guide avoids inventing Sendbird compatible methods and lists related OpenIM signatures. OpenIM model Build scheduled sending in a trusted backend worker. When the schedule fires, the backend should send through a server side integration or authorized service account. Support status: there is no public WASM SDK API equivalent to the Sendbird feature named by this page. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Do not call non existent Sendbird style methods from the OpenIM client; model the feature with the alternatives below. API signatures No public OpenIM WASM SDK method directly implements this Sendbird feature. Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; createTextMessage: text: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; createCustomMessage: params: CustomMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Schedule in the backend and send when the job fires. await backend.scheduleMessage conversationID, recvID, groupID, text, sendAt: scheduledAt.toISOString , ; The browser can render schedule state returned by your backend, but it should not be the scheduler of record. Returned data and events When the page describes a partial, server side, or unsupported Sendbird feature, treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks. Keep the authoritative product state in your backend when the SDK does not own it. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Confirm your UI does not expose a control that implies native SDK support unless the backend alternative is implemented. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model Mention messages are group messages with an atUserIDList . OpenIM conversation fields track whether the current user was mentioned. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. title: Overview
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for Overview, with API signatures, usage details, and verification steps.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm message overview-message
content: Overview Use message APIs to create, send, receive, search, mark read, delete, revoke, and enrich OpenIM chat messages from the browser SDK. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model OpenIM creates a typed MessageItem first, sends it to a direct receiver or group, then keeps local state fresh with message, receipt, revoke, and conversation events. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; createTextMessage: text: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; createAdvancedTextMessage: params: AdvancedMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; createTextAtMessage: data: AtMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; createImageMessageByFile: params: ImageMsgParamsByURL & file: File , operationID?: string = Promise ; createFileMessageByFile: data: FileMsgParamsByURL & file: File , operationID?: string = Promise ; createCustomMessage: params: CustomMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getAdvancedHistoryMessageList: params: GetAdvancedHistoryMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; searchLocalMessages: data: SearchLocalParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; markConversationMessageAsRead: data: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessage CbEvents.OnRecvC2CReadReceipt CbEvents.OnRecvGroupReadReceipt CbEvents.OnNewRecvMessageRevoked CbEvents.OnProgress CbEvents.UploadComplete Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Use the same pipeline for most message types: create, send, listen, and load history. const data: message = await OpenIM.createTextMessage text ; const data: sent = await OpenIM.sendMessage recvID, groupID: '', message ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages, data = mergeMessages data ; const data: history = await OpenIM.getAdvancedHistoryMessageList conversationID, startClientMsgID: '', count: 30, viewType: 0, ; Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages , CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessage , CbEvents.OnRecvC2CReadReceipt , CbEvents.OnRecvGroupReadReceipt , CbEvents.OnNewRecvMessageRevoked , CbEvents.OnProgress , CbEvents.UploadComplete . Always log operationID with failures. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm refresh and reconnect behavior matches the product requirement. title: Smart throttling
description: Server-side implementation guidance and WASM SDK boundaries for Smart throttling.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm message sending-a-message smart-throttling
content: Overview Spam protection and smart throttling are service side policies, not public WASM SDK APIs. This guide marks server owned behavior clearly and lists related browser SDK signatures. OpenIM model Keep abuse prevention in the backend or gateway. The browser should debounce UI actions and render server side failures without assuming delivery. Support status: the browser SDK can provide context, but the authoritative action should run on your backend or Platform API layer. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Provide a trusted backend endpoint when the action affects moderation, push, reporting, scheduled jobs, or admin behavior. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; createTextMessage: text: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages CbEvents.OnSyncServerFailed Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Use browser throttles only as UX protection. if sendButtonLocked return; const decision = await backend.checkCanSend conversationID ; if decision.allowed throw new Error decision.reason ; const data: message = await OpenIM.createTextMessage text ; await OpenIM.sendMessage recvID, groupID, message ; Returned data and events When the page describes a partial, server side, or unsupported Sendbird feature, treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks. Keep the authoritative product state in your backend when the SDK does not own it. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages , CbEvents.OnSyncServerFailed . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Confirm the backend validates the current user and does not trust IDs or role claims from the browser alone. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | | A Sendbird method name is missing | The feature is not part of the OpenIM WASM SDK public API. | Use the OpenIM alternative on this page or implement the feature in a trusted backend. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

Support status: the browser SDK can provide context, but the authoritative action should run on your backend or Platform API layer. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Provide a trusted backend endpoint when the action affects moderation, push, reporting, scheduled jobs, or admin behavior. title: Create a message thread
description: OpenIM WASM SDK mapping, API signatures, and implementation notes for Create a message thread.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm message using-message-threading create-a-message-thread
content: Overview OpenIM WASM SDK does not expose Sendbird message thread objects. Use quote messages or a custom backend thread model when replies need a separate timeline. This guide separates public OpenIM SDK support from product behavior that needs application code. OpenIM model Quote messages reference an existing message in the main conversation. They are not equivalent to threaded reply collections. Support status: the WASM SDK covers part of this workflow; the remaining behavior belongs in your application or backend. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Have a target conversationID , recvID , or groupID depending on whether the flow is direct or group chat. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; createQuoteMessage: params: QuoteMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; createAdvancedQuoteMessage: params: AdvancedQuoteMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; createCustomMessage: params: CustomMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; fetchSurroundingMessages: params: FetchSurroundingParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Use quote messages for inline replies. Use a backend if replies need a separate thread list. const data: reply = await OpenIM.createAdvancedQuoteMessage text: 'Replying to this', message: parentMessage, ; await OpenIM.sendMessage recvID: '', groupID, message: reply ; Returned data and events When the page describes a partial, server side, or unsupported Sendbird feature, treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks. Keep the authoritative product state in your backend when the SDK does not own it. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Confirm the application owned part of the feature survives refresh, reconnect, and a second device session. title: OpenIM SDK for WASM
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for OpenIM SDK for WASM, with API signatures, usage details, and verification steps.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm overview
content: Overview Use this page as the entry point for the OpenIM WASM SDK. It explains the browser package, WASM assets, authentication flow, message flow, and where each Sendbird style concept maps in OpenIM. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model OpenIM WASM SDK uses a single SDK instance returned by getSDK . Conversations are addressed by conversationID ; direct messages use recvID ; group messages use groupID ; groups cover the Sendbird group channel style workflow. The browser package is backed by WASM and persists local state in IndexedDB through sql.js. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; login: params: InitAndLoginConfig, operationID?: string = Promise ; logout: operationID?: string = Promise ; createTextMessage: text: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getAllConversationList: operationID?: string = Promise ; getAdvancedHistoryMessageList: params: GetAdvancedHistoryMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; createGroup: data: CreateGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getUsersInfo: data: string , operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnConnecting CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess CbEvents.OnConnectFailed CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages CbEvents.OnConversationChanged CbEvents.OnUserStatusChanged Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Create and reuse one SDK instance in the browser session. import CbEvents, getSDK from '@openim/wasm client sdk'; const OpenIM = getSDK coreWasmPath: '/openIM.wasm', sqlWasmPath: '/sql wasm.wasm', ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess, = console.info 'OpenIM connected' ; ; await OpenIM.login userID, token, platformID: 5, apiAddr, wsAddr, ; Use these SDK areas as the main navigation model for WASM docs: Application: initialize, authenticate, reconnect, and handle tokens. Channel: use groups and conversations for channel like UI. Message: create a message, send it, receive events, load history, and mark read. User: retrieve profiles, presence, friendships, blacklist, and group member state. Local cache: query conversations/messages and merge SDK events into your own store. Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnConnecting , CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess , CbEvents.OnConnectFailed , CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages , CbEvents.OnConversationChanged , CbEvents.OnUserStatusChanged . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

Returned data and events Treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks when the feature is partial or server owned. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages , CbEvents.OnTotalUnreadMessageCountChanged . Always log operationID with failures. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm refresh and reconnect behavior matches the product requirement. Confirm the backend validates user identity, role, and target IDs instead of trusting browser claims. content: Overview Friend requests are the workflow for creating a friendship when your product requires approval. OpenIM model Sent and received friend applications are represented by FriendApplicationItem . Prerequisites Log in as the current user. Prepare the target toUserID for a new request. API signatures addFriend: data: AddFriendParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getFriendApplicationListAsRecipient: data?: GetFriendApplicationListAsRecipientParams, operationID?: string, = Promise ; getFriendApplicationListAsApplicant: data?: GetFriendApplicationListAsApplicationParams, operationID?: string, = Promise ; getFriendApplicationUnhandledCount: data: GetSelfUnhandledApplyCountParams, operationID?: string, = Promise ; acceptFriendApplication: data: AccessFriendApplicationParams, operationID?: string, = Promise ; refuseFriendApplication: data: AccessFriendApplicationParams, operationID?: string, = Promise ; Implementation await OpenIM.addFriend toUserID: 'user b', reqMsg: 'Hi' ; const data: received = await OpenIM.getFriendApplicationListAsRecipient handleResults: 0 , offset: 0, count: 20, ; await OpenIM.acceptFriendApplication toUserID: 'user a', handleMsg: 'Approved', ; Returned data and events Watch CbEvents.OnFriendApplicationAdded , CbEvents.OnFriendApplicationAccepted , CbEvents.OnFriendApplicationRejected , and CbEvents.OnFriendAdded . Verify the result Confirm the recipient receives a pending application. Confirm accepting the request creates a friend relationship for both users. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | No pending request | The wrong list direction was queried | Use recipient list for requests sent to the current user. | Next steps Retrieve friend information /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/retrieving users/retrieve friend information

title: Update or delete friends

content: Overview Use updateFriends to update relationship fields and deleteFriend to remove a friend relationship. OpenIM model These methods operate on the current user's relationship with target users. Prerequisites Log in as the relationship owner. Prepare the target friend user IDs. API signatures updateFriends: data: UpdateFriendsParams, operationID?: string, = Promise ; deleteFriend: data: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; Implementation await OpenIM.updateFriends friendUserIDs: 'user b' , remark: 'Alex', isPinned: true, ex: JSON.stringify source: 'crm' , ; await OpenIM.deleteFriend 'user b' ; Returned data and events Refresh the contact list when CbEvents.OnFriendInfoChanged or CbEvents.OnFriendDeleted fires. Verify the result Confirm remarks and pinned state appear after reload. Confirm deleting a friend removes the relationship from the list. title: Authentication
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for Authentication, with API signatures, usage details, and verification steps.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm application authenticating-a-user authentication
content: Overview Authenticate a WASM user by creating the SDK instance, registering connection listeners, calling login , and handling token lifecycle events. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model Browser clients should receive userID and token from a trusted backend. login starts the session; the connection callbacks tell the UI when the WebSocket is actually ready. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; login: params: InitAndLoginConfig, operationID?: string = Promise ; logout: operationID?: string = Promise ; getLoginStatus: operationID?: string = Promise ; getLoginUserID: operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnConnecting CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess CbEvents.OnConnectFailed CbEvents.OnKickedOffline CbEvents.OnUserTokenExpired CbEvents.OnUserTokenInvalid Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Create and reuse one SDK instance in the browser session. import CbEvents, getSDK from '@openim/wasm client sdk'; const OpenIM = getSDK coreWasmPath: '/openIM.wasm', sqlWasmPath: '/sql wasm.wasm', ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess, = console.info 'OpenIM connected' ; ; await OpenIM.login userID, token, platformID: 5, apiAddr, wsAddr, ; Register token and kick offline handlers before calling login . OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnUserTokenExpired, requestFreshToken ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnUserTokenInvalid, forceSignOut ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnKickedOffline, showSignedInElsewhereDialog ; await OpenIM.login userID, token, platformID: 5, apiAddr, wsAddr ; const data: loginStatus = await OpenIM.getLoginStatus ; Call logout before switching accounts in the same browser session. Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnConnecting , CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess , CbEvents.OnConnectFailed , CbEvents.OnKickedOffline , CbEvents.OnUserTokenExpired , CbEvents.OnUserTokenInvalid . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

title: Overview
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for Overview, with API signatures, usage details, and verification steps.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm application overview-application
content: Overview Use the application section to initialize the WASM SDK, authenticate a user, watch connection state, handle token lifecycle events, and apply SDK wide session behavior. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model Application state is split between your backend and the browser SDK. The backend creates OpenIM users and tokens. The browser SDK connects to apiAddr and wsAddr , then emits lifecycle events that drive the UI. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; login: params: InitAndLoginConfig, operationID?: string = Promise ; logout: operationID?: string = Promise ; getLoginStatus: operationID?: string = Promise ; getLoginUserID: operationID?: string = Promise ; setAppBackgroundStatus: data: boolean, operationID?: string = Promise ; networkStatusChanged: operationID?: string = Promise ; setGlobalRecvMessageOpt: opt: MessageReceiveOptType, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnConnecting CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess CbEvents.OnConnectFailed CbEvents.OnKickedOffline CbEvents.OnUserTokenExpired CbEvents.OnUserTokenInvalid Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Register connection and token handlers before login, then let the rest of the app wait for connected state. const OpenIM = getSDK coreWasmPath: '/openIM.wasm', sqlWasmPath: '/sql wasm.wasm', ; await OpenIM.login userID, token, platformID: 5, apiAddr, wsAddr ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnUserTokenExpired, refreshTokenAndReconnect ; const data: loginStatus = await OpenIM.getLoginStatus ; const data: currentUserID = await OpenIM.getLoginUserID ; Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnConnecting , CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess , CbEvents.OnConnectFailed , CbEvents.OnKickedOffline , CbEvents.OnUserTokenExpired , CbEvents.OnUserTokenInvalid . Always log operationID with failures. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm refresh and reconnect behavior matches the product requirement. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

title: Rate limits
description: Server-side implementation guidance and WASM SDK boundaries for Rate limits.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm application understanding-rate-limits rate-limits
content: Overview Rate limiting is a deployment and backend policy concern. The WASM SDK surfaces failures through normal response and event error fields. This guide marks server owned behavior clearly and lists related browser SDK signatures. OpenIM model Do not implement product rate limits only in browser code. Use server policy, gateway limits, and backend checks; then handle OpenIM SDK errors in the UI. Support status: the browser SDK can provide context, but the authoritative action should run on your backend or Platform API layer. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Provide a trusted backend endpoint when the action affects moderation, push, reporting, scheduled jobs, or admin behavior. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; login: params: InitAndLoginConfig, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; createGroup: data: CreateGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; addFriend: data: AddFriendParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnConnectFailed CbEvents.OnSyncServerFailed Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Throttle buttons in the UI, but enforce limits on the backend. try const data: message = await OpenIM.createTextMessage text ; await OpenIM.sendMessage recvID, groupID: '', message ; catch error showRateLimitOrPermissionError error ; For production limits, check the current user, target, and operation on a trusted service before allowing high risk actions. Returned data and events When the page describes a partial, server side, or unsupported Sendbird feature, treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks. Keep the authoritative product state in your backend when the SDK does not own it. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnConnectFailed , CbEvents.OnSyncServerFailed . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Confirm the backend validates the current user and does not trust IDs or role claims from the browser alone. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | | A Sendbird method name is missing | The feature is not part of the OpenIM WASM SDK public API. | Use the OpenIM alternative on this page or implement the feature in a trusted backend. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

content: Overview OpenIM WASM SDK does not expose a Sendbird open channel object or enter/exit API. This guide avoids inventing Sendbird compatible methods and lists related OpenIM signatures. OpenIM model Use OpenIM groups for member based chat rooms, or build public room discovery and access policy in your backend before calling group APIs. Support status: there is no public WASM SDK API equivalent to the Sendbird feature named by this page. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Do not call non existent Sendbird style methods from the OpenIM client; model the feature with the alternatives below. API signatures No public OpenIM WASM SDK method directly implements this Sendbird feature. Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; createGroup: data: CreateGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; joinGroup: data: JoinGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; quitGroup: data: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; getJoinedGroupList: operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupAdded CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupDeleted CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Do not port Sendbird open channel calls one for one. Model public rooms as groups or own discovery in your backend. const data: groups = await OpenIM.searchGroups keywordList: 'public' , isSearchGroupID: false, isSearchGroupName: true, ; await OpenIM.joinGroup groupID: groups 0 .groupID, reqMsg: 'Join public room', joinSource: 3 ; Returned data and events When the page describes a partial, server side, or unsupported Sendbird feature, treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks. Keep the authoritative product state in your backend when the SDK does not own it. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupAdded , CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupDeleted , CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Confirm your UI does not expose a control that implies native SDK support unless the backend alternative is implemented. content: Overview Join or leave an OpenIM group and keep the local channel list synchronized through group and conversation events. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model Joining applies to OpenIM groups. Direct conversations do not have a join step. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. title: Manage channel metacounters

content: Overview OpenIM WASM SDK has no metacounter API. Store counter like data in your backend and mirror display hints through group or conversation extension fields when needed. This guide separates public OpenIM SDK support from product behavior that needs application code. OpenIM model OpenIM exposes string extension fields such as GroupItem.ex and ConversationItem.ex , but atomic counters should be owned by a backend service. Support status: the WASM SDK covers part of this workflow; the remaining behavior belongs in your application or backend. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Keep the authoritative product state in your backend when the SDK does not own it. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnGroupInfoChanged , CbEvents.OnConversationChanged . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Use setGroupMemberInfo for role changes; deprecated role wrappers should not be used in new docs. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. content: Overview OpenIM WASM SDK does not expose a Sendbird open channel object or enter/exit API. This guide avoids inventing Sendbird compatible methods and lists related OpenIM signatures. OpenIM model Use OpenIM groups for member based chat rooms, or build public room discovery and access policy in your backend before calling group APIs. Support status: there is no public WASM SDK API equivalent to the Sendbird feature named by this page. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Do not call non existent Sendbird style methods from the OpenIM client; model the feature with the alternatives below. API signatures No public OpenIM WASM SDK method directly implements this Sendbird feature. Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; createGroup: data: CreateGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; joinGroup: data: JoinGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; quitGroup: data: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; getJoinedGroupList: operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupAdded CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupDeleted CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Do not port Sendbird open channel calls one for one. Model public rooms as groups or own discovery in your backend. const data: groups = await OpenIM.searchGroups keywordList: 'public' , isSearchGroupID: false, isSearchGroupName: true, ; await OpenIM.joinGroup groupID: groups 0 .groupID, reqMsg: 'Join public room', joinSource: 3 ; Returned data and events When the page describes a partial, server side, or unsupported Sendbird feature, treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks. Keep the authoritative product state in your backend when the SDK does not own it. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupAdded , CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupDeleted , CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; login: params: InitAndLoginConfig, operationID?: string = Promise ; logout: operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnConnecting CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess CbEvents.OnConnectFailed CbEvents.OnKickedOffline CbEvents.OnUserTokenExpired CbEvents.OnUserTokenInvalid Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Create and reuse one SDK instance in the browser session. import CbEvents, getSDK from '@openim/wasm client sdk'; const OpenIM = getSDK coreWasmPath: '/openIM.wasm', sqlWasmPath: '/sql wasm.wasm', ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess, = console.info 'OpenIM connected' ; ; await OpenIM.login userID, token, platformID: 5, apiAddr, wsAddr, ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnConnecting, = setConnectionState 'connecting' ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess, = setConnectionState 'connected' ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnConnectFailed, errCode, errMsg = setConnectionError errCode, errMsg ; ; Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnConnecting , CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess , CbEvents.OnConnectFailed , CbEvents.OnKickedOffline , CbEvents.OnUserTokenExpired , CbEvents.OnUserTokenInvalid . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. title: Add or remove a user event handler
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for Add or remove a user event handler, with API signatures, usage details, and verification steps.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm event-handler managing-user-event-handlers add-or-remove-a-user-event-handler
content: Overview Add and remove OpenIM user, friend, blacklist, and online status event handlers. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model User events update profile, relationship, and presence state. Subscribe to online status only for users your UI actually displays. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; getSelfUserInfo: operationID?: string = Promise ; subscribeUsersStatus: data: string , operationID?: string = Promise ; getFriendList: filterBlack?: boolean, operationID?: string = Promise ; getBlackList: operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnSelfInfoUpdated CbEvents.OnUserStatusChanged CbEvents.OnFriendAdded CbEvents.OnFriendDeleted CbEvents.OnFriendInfoChanged CbEvents.OnBlackAdded CbEvents.OnBlackDeleted Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Subscribe to user and relationship events that affect visible UI. OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnSelfInfoUpdated, data = setCurrentUser data ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnUserStatusChanged, data = mergePresence data ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnBlackAdded, data = addBlockedUser data ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnFriendInfoChanged, data = updateFriend data ; Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnSelfInfoUpdated , CbEvents.OnUserStatusChanged , CbEvents.OnFriendAdded , CbEvents.OnFriendDeleted , CbEvents.OnFriendInfoChanged , CbEvents.OnBlackAdded , CbEvents.OnBlackDeleted . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. content: Overview Use event handlers to keep OpenIM WASM SDK UI state synchronized with connection, message, conversation, user, friend, group, and signaling changes. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model The SDK instance returned by getSDK extends an event emitter. Register connection and sync listeners before login and remove long lived page listeners during cleanup. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. title: Environment-specific implementation
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for Environment-specific implementation, with API signatures, usage details, and verification steps.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm getting-started environment-specific-implementation
content: Overview Set up OpenIM client SDKs by matching the runtime to the SDK mode. Browser and renderer layers use WASM assets, while packaged Electron application side integrations can use FFI. Products that support both a web client and an Electron app should usually plan for both modes behind one application level IM adapter. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model The browser SDK requires openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package. Serve them from a stable public path and pass that path to getSDK . Electron integrations need an additional runtime decision. Use WASM for web compatible renderer code and use FFI for application side packaged desktop integration. When both modes exist in the same product, decide which side owns login, connection state, message events, and local state before wiring SDK calls into UI components. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; login: params: InitAndLoginConfig, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnConnecting CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess CbEvents.OnConnectFailed Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Install the package and copy the browser assets during your app build. npm install @openim/wasm client sdk mkdir p public cp node modules/@openim/wasm client sdk/assets/openIM.wasm public/ cp node modules/@openim/wasm client sdk/assets/sql wasm.wasm public/ cp node modules/@openim/wasm client sdk/assets/wasm exec.js public/ Load wasm exec.js before creating the SDK instance. Create and reuse one SDK instance in the browser session. import CbEvents, getSDK from '@openim/wasm client sdk'; const OpenIM = getSDK coreWasmPath: '/openIM.wasm', sqlWasmPath: '/sql wasm.wasm', ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess, = console.info 'OpenIM connected' ; ; await OpenIM.login userID, token, platformID: 5, apiAddr, wsAddr, ; Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnConnecting , CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess , CbEvents.OnConnectFailed . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

title: Local caching
description: OpenIM WASM SDK mapping, API signatures, and implementation notes for Local caching.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm local-caching overview-local-caching
content: Overview Use OpenIM local storage through public conversation, message history, search, sync, and local delete APIs instead of Sendbird collection classes. This guide separates public OpenIM SDK support from application owned behavior and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model The browser SDK stores local state in IndexedDB through sql.js. Public APIs expose cached data as conversations, message history, and search results; internal database modules are not exported from the package entrypoint. Support status: the WASM SDK covers part of this workflow; the remaining behavior belongs in your application or backend. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Keep application owned behavior in your app or backend when the SDK does not expose a direct method. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; getConversationListSplit: data: SplitConversationParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getAdvancedHistoryMessageList: params: GetAdvancedHistoryMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; searchLocalMessages: data: SearchLocalParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; findMessageList: data: FindMessageParams , operationID?: string = Promise ; deleteMessageFromLocalStorage: data: AccessMessageParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; deleteAllConversationFromLocal: operationID?: string = Promise ; exportDB operationID?: string : Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnSyncServerStart CbEvents.OnSyncServerProgress CbEvents.OnSyncServerFinish CbEvents.OnSyncServerFailed CbEvents.OnConversationChanged CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Load an initial page and merge later SDK events into your own store. const data: conversations = await OpenIM.getConversationListSplit offset: 0, count: 30 ; const data: page = await OpenIM.getAdvancedHistoryMessageList conversationID, startClientMsgID: '', count: 30, viewType: 0, ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnSyncServerFinish, refreshVisibleConversation ; Use exportDB only for explicit diagnostic or migration flows. Returned data and events Treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks when the feature is partial or server owned. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnSyncServerStart , CbEvents.OnSyncServerProgress , CbEvents.OnSyncServerFinish , CbEvents.OnSyncServerFailed , CbEvents.OnConversationChanged , CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages . Always log operationID with failures. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm refresh and reconnect behavior matches the product requirement. Confirm the application owned state survives refresh and a second signed in device. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | | A Sendbird method name is missing | The feature is not part of the OpenIM WASM SDK public API. | Use the OpenIM alternative on this page or implement the feature in a trusted backend. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

Build the same UI behavior by loading a page of data and merging subsequent SDK events into your application state. Support status: the WASM SDK covers part of this workflow; the remaining behavior belongs in your application or backend. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Keep the authoritative product state in your backend when the SDK does not own it. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnConversationChanged , CbEvents.OnNewConversation , CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages , CbEvents.OnSyncServerFinish . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users.
