title: Logger
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for Logger, with API signatures, usage details, and verification steps.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm logger
content: Overview Configure SDK logging at login time and use event payloads to capture enough context for browser side diagnostics. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model The WASM SDK exposes logging options through InitAndLoginConfig . The npm type definitions include OnUploadLogsProgress , but there is no public uploadLogs method in the WASM SDK surface, so app level log export should stay in your own diagnostics layer. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . title: Insert a local message
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for inserting single-chat or group-chat messages into local storage.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm message managing-a-message insert-a-local-message
content: Overview Use local insertion only for product controlled system rows, migration, or client side hints that should not be delivered as normal chat messages. OpenIM model insertSingleMessageToLocalStorage and insertGroupMessageToLocalStorage write to local storage. They do not send a message to other users. Prerequisites Create a valid MessageItem . Know whether the row belongs to a single chat or a group chat. API signatures insertSingleMessageToLocalStorage: data: InsertSingleMsgParams, operationID?: string, = Promise ; insertGroupMessageToLocalStorage: data: InsertGroupMsgParams, operationID?: string, = Promise ; Implementation const data: message = await OpenIM.createTextMessage 'Local note' ; await OpenIM.insertSingleMessageToLocalStorage message, recvID: 'user b', sendID: currentUserID, ; Returned data and events Reload the related conversation from local storage after insertion. Verify the result Confirm no remote receiver gets a new message event. Confirm the local conversation renders the inserted row. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | Message appears in the wrong conversation | Wrong recvID , groupID , or sendID | Keep single chat and group chat insert paths separate. | Next steps Retrieve a list of messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/retrieving messages/retrieve a list of messages

title: Update a message
description: OpenIM WASM SDK mapping, API signatures, and implementation notes for Update a message.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm message managing-a-message update-a-message
content: Overview OpenIM WASM SDK does not expose a general edit message API. Use revoke and resend, custom messages, or local extension fields depending on the requirement. This guide separates public OpenIM SDK support from product behavior that needs application code. OpenIM model setMessageLocalEx updates local client metadata only. It is not a server side message edit. Support status: the WASM SDK covers part of this workflow; the remaining behavior belongs in your application or backend. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. content: Overview The OpenIM WASM SDK does not expose poll, poll option, vote, voter, or poll changelog APIs. This guide avoids inventing Sendbird compatible methods and lists related OpenIM signatures. OpenIM model Implement polls as a backend feature and optionally publish poll state changes as OpenIM custom messages. Support status: there is no public WASM SDK API equivalent to the Sendbird feature named by this page. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Do not call non existent Sendbird style methods from the OpenIM client; model the feature with the alternatives below. API signatures No public OpenIM WASM SDK method directly implements this Sendbird feature. Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; createCustomMessage: params: CustomMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; searchLocalMessages: data: SearchLocalParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnRecvCustomBusinessMessage CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Model polls as backend records and publish state changes as custom messages. const poll = await backend.createPoll groupID, question, options ; const data: message = await OpenIM.createCustomMessage data: JSON.stringify type: 'poll', pollID: poll.id , extension: 'poll', description: 'Poll', ; await OpenIM.sendMessage recvID: '', groupID, message ; Returned data and events When the page describes a partial, server side, or unsupported Sendbird feature, treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks. Keep the authoritative product state in your backend when the SDK does not own it. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnRecvCustomBusinessMessage , CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. content: Overview OpenIM WASM SDK has no public scheduled message create, update, cancel, list, or send now API. This guide avoids inventing Sendbird compatible methods and lists related OpenIM signatures. OpenIM model Build scheduled sending in a trusted backend worker. When the schedule fires, the backend should send through a server side integration or authorized service account. Support status: there is no public WASM SDK API equivalent to the Sendbird feature named by this page. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. title: Receive messages in a group channel
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for Receive messages in a group channel, with API signatures, usage details, and verification steps.

content: Overview Receive OpenIM group messages through event handlers and merge them into the active conversation UI. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model Use OnRecvNewMessages for normal UI updates. Load history separately for pagination and reconnect recovery. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Have a target conversationID , recvID , or groupID depending on whether the flow is direct or group chat. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; getAdvancedHistoryMessageList: params: GetAdvancedHistoryMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; markConversationMessageAsRead: data: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessage CbEvents.OnRecvOfflineNewMessages CbEvents.OnRecvOnlineOnlyMessages CbEvents.OnNewRecvMessageRevoked Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Register message handlers once after creating the SDK instance. OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages, data = mergeMessages data ; ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnNewRecvMessageRevoked, data = markMessageRevoked data ; ; await OpenIM.markConversationMessageAsRead conversationID ; Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages , CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessage , CbEvents.OnRecvOfflineNewMessages , CbEvents.OnRecvOnlineOnlyMessages , CbEvents.OnNewRecvMessageRevoked . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | | Messages do not appear in the UI | The listener was registered too late, the wrong recvID or groupID was used, or history has not synced. | Register message events before testing and verify the target IDs with a second signed in user. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . content: Overview Retrieve total and conversation level unread state directly; use group read receipt APIs for member level read/unread lists. This guide separates public OpenIM SDK support from product behavior that needs application code. OpenIM model OpenIM stores unread count on conversations and exposes global unread totals. Group message reader APIs can list users by read status for a specific message when receipts are enabled. Support status: the WASM SDK covers part of this workflow; the remaining behavior belongs in your application or backend. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Have a target conversationID , recvID , or groupID depending on whether the flow is direct or group chat. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; getTotalUnreadMsgCount: operationID?: string = Promise ; getAllConversationList: operationID?: string = Promise ; getConversationListSplit: data: SplitConversationParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getGroupMessageReaderList: params: GetGroupMessageReaderParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnTotalUnreadMessageCountChanged CbEvents.OnConversationChanged CbEvents.OnRecvGroupReadReceipt Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Use conversation data and unread count callbacks. const data: totalUnread = await OpenIM.getTotalUnreadMsgCount ; const data: conversations = await OpenIM.getAllConversationList ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnTotalUnreadMessageCountChanged, data = setBadgeCount data ; ; For a specific group message, query reader lists with filter 0 for read or 1 for unread. Returned data and events When the page describes a partial, server side, or unsupported Sendbird feature, treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks. Keep the authoritative product state in your backend when the SDK does not own it. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnTotalUnreadMessageCountChanged , CbEvents.OnConversationChanged , CbEvents.OnRecvGroupReadReceipt . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Confirm the application owned part of the feature survives refresh, reconnect, and a second device session. title: Cancel an in-progress file upload

content: Overview The OpenIM WASM SDK does not expose a public cancel upload method. Model cancellation in your app queue before handing the file to the SDK, and reconcile late SDK progress events. This guide separates public OpenIM SDK support from application owned behavior and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model Once a file message is passed to create MessageByFile and sendMessage , the public SDK surface has progress and completion events but no cancel token. Your UI can cancel queued work before SDK submission or ignore a late result after the user cancels. Support status: the WASM SDK covers part of this workflow; the remaining behavior belongs in your application or backend. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Keep application owned behavior in your app or backend when the SDK does not expose a direct method. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; createFileMessageByFile: data: FileMsgParamsByURL & file: File , operationID?: string = Promise ; createImageMessageByFile: params: ImageMsgParamsByURL & file: File , operationID?: string = Promise ; createVideoMessageByFile: data: VideoMsgParamsByURL & videoFile: File; snapshotFile: File , operationID?: string, = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; fileMapSet: uuid: string, file: File = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnProgress CbEvents.UploadComplete Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Keep a queue item state around file sends and check cancellation before each SDK boundary. const uploadJob = id: crypto.randomUUID , cancelled: false, file ; if uploadJob.cancelled return; const data: message = await OpenIM.createFileMessageByFile file, filePath: file.name, fileName: file.name, uuid: uploadJob.id, sourceUrl: '', fileSize: file.size, fileType: file.type, ; if uploadJob.cancelled return; await OpenIM.sendMessage recvID: '', groupID, message ; If the SDK has already accepted the send, hide or mark the UI item as cancelled locally and let the final success/failure event settle. Returned data and events Treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks when the feature is partial or server owned. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnProgress , CbEvents.UploadComplete . Always log operationID with failures. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm refresh and reconnect behavior matches the product requirement. title: Send an admin message
description: Server-side implementation guidance and WASM SDK boundaries for Send an admin message.

content: Overview Admin messages should be created by a trusted backend or Platform API workflow, not by an untrusted browser client. This guide marks server owned behavior clearly and lists related browser SDK signatures. OpenIM model If the UI must display system content, send a custom message from a trusted service account and validate it in the client. Support status: the browser SDK can provide context, but the authoritative action should run on your backend or Platform API layer. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Provide a trusted backend endpoint when the action affects moderation, push, reporting, scheduled jobs, or admin behavior. Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. title: Migrating to OpenIM

content: Overview Migrate Sendbird style Web chat code by replacing app/channel/user/message primitives with OpenIM users, conversations, groups, messages, and event callbacks. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model OpenIM does not implement Sendbird collections, open channels, polls, scheduled messages, or reactions as equivalent client SDK objects. Keep those as backend or custom message features during migration. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; login: params: InitAndLoginConfig, operationID?: string = Promise ; getConversationIDBySessionType: data: GetOneConversationParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; createGroup: data: CreateGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getAdvancedHistoryMessageList: params: GetAdvancedHistoryMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; searchLocalMessages: data: SearchLocalParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages CbEvents.OnConversationChanged CbEvents.OnNewConversation CbEvents.OnTotalUnreadMessageCountChanged Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Migrate by concept, not by method name. const OpenIM = getSDK coreWasmPath: '/openIM.wasm', sqlWasmPath: '/sql wasm.wasm', ; await OpenIM.login userID, token, platformID: 5, apiAddr, wsAddr ; const data: conversations = await OpenIM.getAllConversationList ; const data: message = await OpenIM.createTextMessage 'Migrated hello' ; await OpenIM.sendMessage recvID, groupID: '', message ; Replace Sendbird open channels, polls, scheduled messages, reactions, and collection APIs with explicit OpenIM group/conversation/message APIs plus backend services where needed. Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages , CbEvents.OnConversationChanged , CbEvents.OnNewConversation , CbEvents.OnTotalUnreadMessageCountChanged . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. title: Collection migration guide
description: OpenIM WASM SDK mapping, API signatures, and implementation notes for Collection migration guide.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm migration-guide collection-migration-guide
content: Overview Replace Sendbird collection objects with OpenIM conversation/message queries plus event handlers that update your own store. This guide separates public OpenIM SDK support from product behavior that needs application code. OpenIM model OpenIM does not expose GroupChannelCollection or MessageCollection as WASM SDK classes. Build the same UI behavior by loading a page of data and merging subsequent SDK events into your application state. Support status: the WASM SDK covers part of this workflow; the remaining behavior belongs in your application or backend. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; getConversationListSplit: data: SplitConversationParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getAdvancedHistoryMessageList: params: GetAdvancedHistoryMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; searchLocalMessages: data: SearchLocalParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; findMessageList: data: FindMessageParams , operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnConversationChanged CbEvents.OnNewConversation CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages CbEvents.OnSyncServerFinish Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Create your own collection state from query results and events. const state = createMessageStore ; state.replacePage await OpenIM.getAdvancedHistoryMessageList conversationID, startClientMsgID: '', count: 30, viewType: 0, , ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages, data = state.mergeIncoming data ; ; Returned data and events When the page describes a partial, server side, or unsupported Sendbird feature, treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks. Keep the authoritative product state in your backend when the SDK does not own it. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnConversationChanged , CbEvents.OnNewConversation , CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages , CbEvents.OnSyncServerFinish . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Confirm the application owned part of the feature survives refresh, reconnect, and a second device session. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | | A Sendbird method name is missing | The feature is not part of the OpenIM WASM SDK public API. | Use the OpenIM alternative on this page or implement the feature in a trusted backend. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

title: Migration guide
description: OpenIM WASM SDK mapping, API signatures, and implementation notes for Migration guide.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm migration-guide migration-guide
content: Overview Migrate Sendbird Web chat code by replacing app, channel, user, message, collection, and moderation primitives with OpenIM users, conversations, groups, messages, events, and backend owned extensions. This guide separates public OpenIM SDK support from application owned behavior and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model OpenIM covers core chat workflows with public WASM SDK APIs, but it does not provide one to one equivalents for Sendbird open channels, polls, scheduled messages, reactions, message collections, or channel metacounters. Support status: the WASM SDK covers part of this workflow; the remaining behavior belongs in your application or backend. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Keep application owned behavior in your app or backend when the SDK does not expose a direct method. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; login: params: InitAndLoginConfig, operationID?: string = Promise ; getConversationIDBySessionType: data: GetOneConversationParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; createGroup: data: CreateGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getAdvancedHistoryMessageList: params: GetAdvancedHistoryMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; searchLocalMessages: data: SearchLocalParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages CbEvents.OnConversationChanged CbEvents.OnNewConversation CbEvents.OnTotalUnreadMessageCountChanged Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Migrate by product concept instead of copying method names. const OpenIM = getSDK coreWasmPath: '/openIM.wasm', sqlWasmPath: '/sql wasm.wasm', ; await OpenIM.login userID, token, platformID: 5, apiAddr, wsAddr ; const data: conversations = await OpenIM.getAllConversationList ; const data: message = await OpenIM.createTextMessage 'Migrated hello' ; await OpenIM.sendMessage recvID, groupID: '', message ; Keep unsupported Sendbird product features behind explicit backend services or OpenIM custom message payloads. Returned data and events Treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks when the feature is partial or server owned. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages , CbEvents.OnConversationChanged , CbEvents.OnNewConversation , CbEvents.OnTotalUnreadMessageCountChanged . Always log operationID with failures. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm refresh and reconnect behavior matches the product requirement. Confirm the application owned state survives refresh and a second signed in device. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | | A Sendbird method name is missing | The feature is not part of the OpenIM WASM SDK public API. | Use the OpenIM alternative on this page or implement the feature in a trusted backend. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

title: Push notification content templates
description: Server-side implementation guidance and WASM SDK boundaries for Push notification content templates.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm push-notifications configuring-preferences push-notification-content-templates
content: Overview Notification content templates are provider and backend behavior, not a public browser SDK method. This guide marks server owned behavior clearly and lists related browser SDK signatures. OpenIM model The public @openim/wasm client sdk surface does not include Web Push, APNs, FCM token registration, notification template, or notification translation methods. Use OpenIM conversation receive options for in app behavior and own provider integration in your backend. Support status: the browser SDK can provide context, but the authoritative action should run on your backend or Platform API layer. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Provide a trusted backend endpoint for authoritative product behavior. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; setConversation: params: SetConversationParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; setConversationRecvMessageOpt: data: SetConversationRecvOptParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getConversationRecvMessageOpt: data: string , operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages CbEvents.OnTotalUnreadMessageCountChanged Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Set in app receive behavior through conversation settings, then use your own service worker and backend for browser push. await OpenIM.setConversation conversationID, recvMsgOpt: 2, ; const data: options = await OpenIM.getConversationRecvMessageOpt conversationID ; Store Web Push subscriptions, localized templates, and provider credentials outside the browser SDK. Returned data and events Treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks when the feature is partial or server owned. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages , CbEvents.OnTotalUnreadMessageCountChanged . Always log operationID with failures. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm refresh and reconnect behavior matches the product requirement. Confirm the backend validates user identity, role, and target IDs instead of trusting browser claims. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | | A Sendbird method name is missing | The feature is not part of the OpenIM WASM SDK public API. | Use the OpenIM alternative on this page or implement the feature in a trusted backend. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

title: Report a message, user, or channel
description: Server-side implementation guidance and WASM SDK boundaries for Report a message, user, or channel.

content: Overview Create reports by collecting message, user, or group IDs in the browser and sending the report to your backend. This guide marks server owned behavior clearly and lists related browser SDK signatures. OpenIM model The WASM SDK can retrieve the message, user, group, and conversation context a report form needs. The authoritative report record, abuse workflow, and moderation decision should be handled by a trusted backend or Platform API layer. Support status: the browser SDK can provide context, but the authoritative action should run on your backend or Platform API layer. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Provide a trusted backend endpoint for authoritative product behavior. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; findMessageList: data: FindMessageParams , operationID?: string = Promise ; getUsersInfo: data: string , operationID?: string = Promise ; getSpecifiedGroupsInfo: params: string , operationID?: string = Promise ; getOneConversation: params: GetOneConversationParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Collect evidence IDs in the client and submit them to the backend. const data: messageResult = await OpenIM.findMessageList conversationID, clientMsgIDList: clientMsgID , ; await backend.createModerationReport conversationID, clientMsgID, targetUserID, groupID, evidence: messageResult, ; Do not trust browser submitted role, membership, or ownership claims without backend validation. Returned data and events Treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks when the feature is partial or server owned. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages . Always log operationID with failures. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm refresh and reconnect behavior matches the product requirement. Confirm the backend validates user identity, role, and target IDs instead of trusting browser claims. content: Overview Block, unblock, and list OpenIM users in the current user's blacklist. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model Blacklist state is user scoped. Keep the UI clear about the difference between blocking a user and moderating a group member. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. content: Overview Mute or unmute OpenIM group members and retrieve member mute state from group member records. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model OpenIM muting is group scoped. muteEndTime on GroupMemberItem indicates whether a member is currently muted. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. title: Overview
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for Overview, with API signatures, usage details, and verification steps.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm user overview-user
content: Overview Use user APIs for current user profile, application user lookup, online status, friendship, and blacklist views. Conversation display names belong to conversation data. Use ConversationItem.showName for conversation lists and direct chat headers instead of filling those titles with getUsersInfo . This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model OpenIM separates account profile, friendship, blacklist, online status, and group membership. Avoid treating every SDK user call as an application wide directory query. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; getSelfUserInfo: operationID?: string = Promise ; setSelfInfo: data: PartialUserItem, operationID?: string = Promise ; getUsersInfo: data: string , operationID?: string = Promise ; subscribeUsersStatus: data: string , operationID?: string = Promise ; getFriendListPage: data: OffsetParams & filterBlack?: boolean , operationID?: string = Promise ; addBlack: data: AddBlackParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnSelfInfoUpdated CbEvents.OnUserStatusChanged CbEvents.OnFriendAdded CbEvents.OnFriendDeleted CbEvents.OnBlackAdded CbEvents.OnBlackDeleted Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Load user state by scope. const data: me = await OpenIM.getSelfUserInfo ; const usersResponse = await OpenIM.getUsersInfo userIDs ; const users = usersResponse.data ?? ; const data: friends = await OpenIM.getFriendListPage offset: 0, count: 50, filterBlack: true, ; const data: blocked = await OpenIM.getBlackList ; Subscribe to presence only for users visible in the current UI. Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnSelfInfoUpdated , CbEvents.OnUserStatusChanged , CbEvents.OnFriendAdded , CbEvents.OnFriendDeleted , CbEvents.OnBlackAdded , CbEvents.OnBlackDeleted . Always log operationID with failures. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm refresh and reconnect behavior matches the product requirement. title: Retrieve users in an application
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for retrieving application user public profiles with getUsersInfo.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm user retrieving-users retrieve-a-list-of-users-in-an-application
content: Overview Use getUsersInfo to retrieve public profile data for application users by userID . This can support flows such as searching by user ID before sending a friend request or opening a public profile card. Do not use getUsersInfo to fill a direct conversation title. OpenIM conversation objects already expose showName ; conversation lists and chat headers should prefer the conversation data. API signatures getUsersInfo: data: string , operationID?: string = Promise ; Implementation try const response = await OpenIM.getUsersInfo 'user a', 'user b' ; const users = response.data ?? ; renderUsers users ; catch error console.error 'getUsersInfo failed', error ; throw error; Returned data The response contains PublicUserItem with userID , nickname , faceURL , and ex . Next steps Retrieve a list of friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/retrieving users/retrieve a list of friends Manage friend requests /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/managing friends/manage friend requests

Refresh the first page when friendship events arrive. title: Retrieve specified friend information
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for retrieving specified friend relationship information and checking friendship state.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm user retrieving-users retrieve-friend-information
content: Overview Use this page when you need relationship data for specified friends, not the whole friend list. OpenIM model Friend information is scoped to the current logged in user and returns FriendUserItem . Prerequisites Log in before calling relationship APIs. Prepare the target friend user IDs. API signatures getSpecifiedFriendsInfo: data: GetSpecifiedFriendsParams, operationID?: string, = Promise ; checkFriend: data: string , operationID?: string, = Promise ; Implementation Fetch relationship details by specified friend user IDs, and use checkFriend when you only need to know whether a relationship exists. const data: friends = await OpenIM.getSpecifiedFriendsInfo friendUserIDList: 'user a', 'user b' , filterBlack: true, ; const data: checks = await OpenIM.checkFriend 'user a', 'user b' ; Returned data and events getSpecifiedFriendsInfo returns FriendUserItem . checkFriend returns FriendshipInfo . Verify the result Confirm each requested friendUserID exists. Confirm blacklisted users are filtered only when filterBlack is enabled. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | Missing users | The target is not a friend or was filtered | Check the friendship state and blacklist setting. | Next steps Retrieve a list of friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/retrieving users/retrieve a list of friends Update or delete friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/managing friends/update or delete friends

title: OpenIM SDK for Electron
description: OpenIM Electron SDK entry point for planning WASM and FFI usage across web-compatible UI and packaged desktop apps.
keywords: sdk electron v4 chat sdk v4 electron overview
content: Overview OpenIM Electron SDK can use both WASM and FFI integration modes. They are not always an either or choice: if the same product supports a web client and a packaged Electron app, you typically need to plan for both. Use WASM for browser pages, renderer processes, WebViews, or UI layers that should stay aligned with the web client. Use FFI for the desktop application side, packaged app behavior, native runtime integration, and boundaries that should live outside a browser like page. SDK mode responsibilities | Mode | Applies to | Notes | | | | | | WASM | Web pages, Electron renderer processes, WebViews | Initialize like the web SDK and publish openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js where the page can load them. | | FFI | Electron application side capabilities and native apps | Follow the Electron FFI SDK installation, packaging, and initialization rules. Do not use browser WASM asset paths for FFI. | | WASM + FFI | Products that support both web and packaged Electron | Wrap IM access behind an app level adapter, select the runtime implementation per environment, and avoid competing logins. | Desktop integration notes If the product supports both web and Electron, define an app level IM adapter before wiring SDK calls into UI code. Use WASM in renderer or web compatible layers, and verify packaged builds can load openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js . Use FFI for application side integration, and design installation, permission, packaging, and update behavior around the packaged desktop app. If both the main process and renderer process need IM state, define an IPC boundary and decide which side owns login, connection, message events, and local state. Keep tokens out of preload scripts and main process logs. Validate websocket connectivity in packaged builds, not only in local dev mode. Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Environment specific implementation /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/environment specific implementation Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message

title: OpenIM SDK for Mini Program
description: OpenIM Mini Program SDK entry point for supported mini program runtimes.
keywords: sdk miniprogram v4 chat sdk v4 miniprogram overview
content: Overview Mini Program integrations follow the WASM compatible core API model for user login, conversations, messages, groups, and events, while the runtime adapter must respect each mini program platform's storage, networking, upload, and websocket restrictions. Compatibility with WASM Use the WASM SDK documentation for shared OpenIM concepts and method level behavior. Mini Program pages should document only the runtime differences: initialization entry, asset handling, request domain configuration, file upload limits, and background lifecycle. Runtime notes Configure allowed request and websocket domains before testing login. Verify local storage quota and cleanup behavior for conversation and message cache. Test file messages with the target platform's upload API and file size limits. Keep token issuance on a trusted backend and pass only short lived credentials to the client. Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview User authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message

title: OpenIM SDK for React Native
description: OpenIM React Native SDK entry point for mobile applications.
keywords: sdk react-native v4 chat sdk v4 react-native overview
content: Overview Use the React Native SDK when your chat experience is built with React Native and needs mobile runtime behavior for connectivity, local cache, media selection, file upload, and push notification handoff. Integration model The SDK should be initialized after your application has a backend issued token for the current userID . Register connection and message events before login, then keep conversation state in your application store so native lifecycle changes can restore the UI predictably. Mobile considerations Validate login, reconnect, and message receipt after app foreground and background transitions. Test file messages with the media picker and upload permissions required by each platform. Route push notification registration through your trusted backend and platform notification services. Record operationID , errCode , and errMsg when diagnosing mobile only failures. Related SDKs iOS SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/ios/overview Android SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/overview Flutter SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/overview

content: OpenIM Platform API 面向可信后端服务，提供用户、群组、消息、上传、Webhook 和运营管理相关的 REST 接口参考。中文文档保留 Platform API 的导航方式，具体能力、接口路径和请求字段以 OpenIM REST 已覆盖的文档为准。 最常用 认证 在调用管理端 REST API 前，后端服务需要确认 API 地址、请求头和管理员 Token 的使用方式。客户端登录所需的用户 Token 也应由可信后端签发。 接入准备 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/prepare to use api 签发会话 Token /docs/chat/platform api/v3/user/managing session tokens/issue a session token 用户管理 通过 OpenIM 用户管理接口创建、更新、查询用户资料，并把注册、注销、权限等业务规则保留在业务系统中。 创建用户 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/user/creating users/create a user 查询用户列表 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/user/listing users/list users 获取用户信息 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/user/listing users/get a user 消息 使用 OpenIM 消息接口从后端发送消息，并结合 Webhook 或业务后端处理审核、通知和扩展行为。 发送消息 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/message/messaging basics/send a message 迁移其他项目到 OpenIM 将历史消息或其他 IM 项目中的消息数据导入 OpenIM 时，后端可以使用 OpenIM 消息接口写入带原始发送时间的消息记录。 迁移消息 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/migration/migrate messages 推荐功能 群组频道 OpenIM 使用群组能力承载群聊场景。文档中的“群组频道”概念在这里映射为 OpenIM 群组、群成员和入群申请。 创建群组频道 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/channel/creating a channel/create a group channel 邀请成员 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/channel/inviting a user/invite as members channel 内容审核 OpenIM 提供群禁言、成员禁言等可直接调用的管理能力，适合由可信后端执行。 屏蔽用户 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/moderation/blocking users/block users 取消屏蔽用户 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/moderation/blocking users/unblock a user 禁言群组成员 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/moderation/muting a user/mute a member in a group channel 解除群组成员禁言 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/moderation/muting a user/unmute a member in a group channel 资源 | 字段 | 值 | | | | | 支持情况 | OpenIM REST 已提供直接接口 | | 产品区域 | OpenIM 服务端 REST API | | 请求模型 | 后端到后端的 HTTP JSON 请求，使用 operationID 串联日志 | | 鉴权方式 | 管理端 REST API 使用 APP 管理员 Token | OpenIM REST API 介绍 https://docs.openim.io/restapi/apis/introduction OpenIM 错误码 https://docs.openim.io/restapi/errCode

title: 接入准备
description: OpenIM Platform API 中文参考：接入准备。说明 OpenIM REST 支持情况、接口结构和后端实现边界。
keywords: platform-api v3 chat platform-api v3 prepare-to-use-api
content: 在调用 OpenIM Platform API 前，先确认基础地址、公共请求头、鉴权 Token 和请求体约定。本文按照 Platform API 的接入准备结构组织，参数和认证方式以 OpenIM REST 文档为准。 基础地址 OpenIM REST 接口使用部署环境中的 API 地址作为基础地址。文档中的 API ADDRESS 需要替换为你的 OpenIM API 网关或服务端地址。 API ADDRESS 例如，接口页中的 POST API ADDRESS /user/get users 表示向当前环境的 /user/get users 端点发起请求。生产环境建议只允许可信后端访问管理端接口，不要把管理员 Token 暴露给客户端。 请求头 OpenIM 管理端 REST API 以 JSON 请求为主。除具体接口另有说明外，请求通常需要携带以下请求头。 | 请求头 | 必填 | 说明 | | | | | | Content Type | 是 | JSON 请求使用 application/json; charset=utf 8 。 | | operationID | 是 | 全局链路追踪 ID。建议每次请求生成独立值，便于串联服务端日志。 | | token | 管理端接口必填 | APP 管理员 Token。创建用户等少数初始化接口可能不需要，具体以接口页为准。 | Multipart 请求 当前保留的 Platform API 页面主要覆盖 OpenIM 已有的 JSON REST 接口。涉及文件或媒体上传时，按具体上传接口要求使用 multipart/form data 或上传服务返回的上传参数；不要在 multipart 请求中强制设置 JSON 的 Content Type 。 如果接口页仍标注 JSON 请求体，则应继续使用 JSON 请求体提交业务字段。 鉴权 后端服务需要先通过 OpenIM 管理端认证能力获取 APP 管理员 Token，再把该 Token 放入后续管理端接口的 token 请求头。Token 应在服务端保存、刷新和轮换，客户端只应拿到为当前用户签发的登录 Token。 常见接入顺序如下： 1. 在服务端配置 OpenIM API 地址和管理员凭据。 2. 调用 OpenIM 管理端认证接口获取 APP 管理员 Token。 3. 使用管理员 Token 调用用户、群组、消息等管理端接口。 4. 当客户端需要登录 OpenIM 时，由服务端调用用户 Token 接口签发会话 Token。 签发会话 Token /docs/chat/platform api/v3/user/managing session tokens/issue a session token OpenIM 管理员 Token 文档 https://docs.openim.io/restapi/apis/authenticationManagement/getAdminToken 请求体 当前保留的 OpenIM Platform API 页面均为 POST 到固定路径，业务参数放在 JSON 请求体中。不要把分页、用户 ID、群组 ID 或消息字段拼接到 URL query。 如果字段值本身是 URL，例如头像地址、文件地址或对象存储地址，作为 JSON 字段提交时保留原始字符串即可。

title: 查询用户列表
description: OpenIM Platform API 中文参考：查询用户列表。说明 OpenIM REST 支持情况、接口结构和后端实现边界。
keywords: platform-api v3 chat platform-api v3 user listing-users list-users
content: 使用 查询用户列表 从可信后端分页读取 OpenIM 已注册用户。请求体中的 pagination.pageNumber 表示页码，从 1 开始； pagination.showNumber 表示每页数量；响应中的 data.total 是用户总数， data.users 是当前页用户列表。 HTTP 请求 POST API ADDRESS /user/get users 请求示例 :::code tabs title="cURL" curl request POST "$ API ADDRESS /user/get users" \ header "Content Type: application/json; charset=utf 8" \ header "operationID: 1646445464564" \ header "token: $ ADMIN TOKEN " \ data raw ' "pagination": "pageNumber": 1, "showNumber": 100 ' title="Node.js" const API ADDRESS = process.env.OPENIM API ADDRESS; const ADMIN TOKEN = process.env.OPENIM ADMIN TOKEN; const response = await fetch $ API ADDRESS /user/get users , method: 'POST', headers: 'Content Type': 'application/json; charset=utf 8', operationID: '1646445464564', token: ADMIN TOKEN , body: JSON.stringify "pagination": "pageNumber": 1, "showNumber": 100 ; const result = await response.json ; if result.errCode == 0 throw new Error result.errMsg || result.errDlt || 'OpenIM request failed' ; title="Go" package main import "fmt" "io" "net/http" "os" "strings" func main payload := strings.NewReader "pagination": "pageNumber": 1, "showNumber": 100 url := os.Getenv "OPENIM API ADDRESS" + "/user/get users" req, err := http.NewRequest http.MethodPost, url, payload if err = nil panic err req.Header.Set "Content Type", "application/json; charset=utf 8" req.Header.Set "operationID", "1646445464564" req.Header.Set "token", os.Getenv "OPENIM ADMIN TOKEN" resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do req if err = nil panic err defer resp.Body.Close body, err := io.ReadAll resp.Body if err = nil panic err fmt.Println string body ::: 安全提示：管理员 Token 只能保存在可信后端服务中，不能下发到客户端或写入前端代码。客户端登录应使用服务端签发的用户 Token。 参数 此接口通过请求头传入链路追踪信息和鉴权凭证，通过 JSON 请求体传递业务参数。 请求头 | 请求头 | 示例值 | 是否必填 | 类型 | 说明 | | : | : | : | | | | operationID | 1646445464564 | 必填 | string | 用于全局链路追踪，建议使用时间戳，在每个请求中独立 | | token | eyJhbxxxx3Xs | 必填 | string | 管理员 token /docs/chat/platform api/v3/prepare to use api | 请求体参数 "pagination": "pageNumber": 1, "showNumber": 100 | 参数名 | 是否必填 | 类型 | 说明 | | : | : | : | | | pagination | 必填 | object | 分页参数结构体 | | pagination.pageNumber | 必填 | int | 当前页码，从 1 开始 | | pagination.showNumber | 必填 | int | 当前页请求数量 | 响应 请求被 OpenIM 正常处理时通常返回 200 OK 。业务是否成功以响应体中的 errCode 为准； errCode === 0 表示成功，非 0 表示业务错误。 "errCode": 0, "errMsg": "", "errDlt": "", "data": "total": 47, "users": "userID": "user 001", "nickname": "Alice", "faceURL": "https://cdn.example.com/avatar/u 001.png", "ex": "", "createTime": 0, "appMangerLevel": 18, "globalRecvMsgOpt": 0 , "userID": "user 002", "nickname": "Bob", "faceURL": "", "ex": "", "createTime": 1688381391965, "appMangerLevel": 18, "globalRecvMsgOpt": 0 响应属性列表 | 参数名 | 类型 | 说明 | | : | : | : | | errCode | int | 错误码，0 表示成功 | | errMsg | string | 错误简要信息，为空 | | errDlt | errDlt | 错误详细信息，为空 | | data | object | 通用数据对象，具体结构见下方 | | total | int | 用户总数 | | users | array | 用户信息 https://docs.openim.io/restapi/commonFields userinfo 列表 | users 属性 | 参数名 | 类型 | 说明 | | | | | | users .userID | string | OpenIM 用户 ID。 | | users .nickname | string | 用户昵称。 | | users .faceURL | string | 用户头像地址。 | | users .ex | string | 业务扩展字段，通常由业务系统写入和解析。 | | users .createTime | int64 | 用户创建时间，通常为毫秒时间戳。 | | users .appMangerLevel | int | OpenIM 返回的应用管理级别字段，字段名以服务端响应为准。 | | users .globalRecvMsgOpt | int | 全局消息接收设置。 | 分页读取建议 从 pagination.pageNumber = 1 开始请求，并用 pagination.showNumber 控制每页数量。响应中的 data.total 表示总用户数，业务后端可以用 Math.ceil data.total / pagination.showNumber 计算总页数，并在当前页码小于总页数时继续请求下一页。 错误 如果请求失败，OpenIM 返回错误对象。更多错误码说明见 错误码 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/error codes 。 "errCode": 1004, "errMsg": "RecordNotFoundError", "errDlt": ": 1004 RecordNotFoundError" | 参数名 | 类型 | 说明 | | : | : | : | | errCode | int | 错误码，具体查看全局错误码文档 | | errMsg | string | 错误简要信息 | | errDlt | errDlt | 错误详细信息 | 常见错误场景 | 错误场景 | 可能原因 | 处理方式 | | | | | | 鉴权失败 | token 缺失、过期，或不是可调用管理端接口的管理员 Token。 | 重新获取 APP 管理员 Token，并只在可信后端保存。 | | 链路追踪困难 | operationID 缺失或在大量请求中重复使用。 | 为每次请求生成独立 operationID ，并在服务端日志中保留。 | | 参数校验失败 | 请求体字段类型、必填字段或枚举值不符合接口要求。 | 对照请求体参数表检查字段类型和必填项。 | | 分页参数错误 | pagination.pageNumber 小于 1，或 pagination.showNumber 超出服务端允许范围。 | 从第 1 页开始读取，并把每页数量限制在服务端允许范围内。 |

title: 获取用户
description: OpenIM Platform API 中文参考：获取用户。说明 OpenIM REST 支持情况、接口结构和后端实现边界。
keywords: platform-api v3 chat platform-api v3 user listing-users get-a-user
content: 使用 获取用户 从可信后端调用 OpenIM REST 接口。请先在 接入准备 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/prepare to use api 中配置 API 地址和管理员 Token；接口参数通过请求头和 JSON 请求体传递。 HTTP 请求 POST API ADDRESS /user/get users info 请求示例 :::code tabs title="cURL" curl request POST "$ API ADDRESS /user/get users info" \ header "Content Type: application/json; charset=utf 8" \ header "operationID: 1646445464564" \ header "token: $ ADMIN TOKEN " \ data raw ' "userIDs": "11111111", "11111112" ' title="Node.js" const API ADDRESS = process.env.OPENIM API ADDRESS; const ADMIN TOKEN = process.env.OPENIM ADMIN TOKEN; const response = await fetch $ API ADDRESS /user/get users info , method: 'POST', headers: 'Content Type': 'application/json; charset=utf 8', operationID: '1646445464564', token: ADMIN TOKEN , body: JSON.stringify "userIDs": "11111111", "11111112" ; const result = await response.json ; if result.errCode == 0 throw new Error result.errMsg || result.errDlt || 'OpenIM request failed' ; title="Go" package main import "fmt" "io" "net/http" "os" "strings" func main payload := strings.NewReader "userIDs": "11111111", "11111112" url := os.Getenv "OPENIM API ADDRESS" + "/user/get users info" req, err := http.NewRequest http.MethodPost, url, payload if err = nil panic err req.Header.Set "Content Type", "application/json; charset=utf 8" req.Header.Set "operationID", "1646445464564" req.Header.Set "token", os.Getenv "OPENIM ADMIN TOKEN" resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do req if err = nil panic err defer resp.Body.Close body, err := io.ReadAll resp.Body if err = nil panic err fmt.Println string body ::: 安全提示：管理员 Token 只能保存在可信后端服务中，不能下发到客户端或写入前端代码。客户端登录应使用服务端签发的用户 Token。 参数 此接口通过请求头传入链路追踪信息和鉴权凭证，通过 JSON 请求体传递业务参数。 请求头 | 请求头 | 示例值 | 是否必填 | 类型 | 说明 | | : | : | : | | | | operationID | 1646445464564 | 必填 | string | 用于全局链路追踪，建议使用时间戳，在每个请求中独立 | | token | eyJhbxxxx3Xs | 必填 | string | 管理员 token /docs/chat/platform api/v3/prepare to use api | 请求体参数 "userIDs": "11111111", "11111112" | 参数名 | 是否必填 | 类型 | 说明 | | : | : | : | | | userIDs | 必填 | array | 需要查询的用户 ID 列表 | 响应 请求被 OpenIM 正常处理时通常返回 200 OK 。业务是否成功以响应体中的 errCode 为准； errCode === 0 表示成功，非 0 表示业务错误。 "errCode": 0, "errMsg": "", "errDlt": "", "data": "usersInfo": "userID": "11111111", "nickname": "yourNickname", "faceURL": "yourFaceURL", "ex": "", "createTime": 1688454168302, "appMangerLevel": 18, "globalRecvMsgOpt": 0 , "userID": "11111112", "nickname": "alantestuid3", "faceURL": "1111111", "ex": "", "createTime": 0, "appMangerLevel": 18, "globalRecvMsgOpt": 0 响应属性列表 | 参数名 | 类型 | 说明 | | : | : | : | | errCode | int | 错误码，0 表示成功 | | errMsg | string | 错误简要信息，为空 | | errDlt | errDlt | 错误详细信息，为空 | | data | object | 通用数据对象，具体结构见下方 | | usersInfo | array | 用户信息 https://docs.openim.io/restapi/commonFields userinfo 列表 | usersInfo 属性 | 参数名 | 类型 | 说明 | | | | | | usersInfo .userID | string | OpenIM 用户 ID。 | | usersInfo .nickname | string | 用户昵称。 | | usersInfo .faceURL | string | 用户头像地址。 | | usersInfo .ex | string | 业务扩展字段，通常由业务系统写入和解析。 | | usersInfo .createTime | int64 | 用户创建时间，通常为毫秒时间戳。 | | usersInfo .appMangerLevel | int | OpenIM 返回的应用管理级别字段，字段名以服务端响应为准。 | | usersInfo .globalRecvMsgOpt | int | 全局消息接收设置。 | 错误 如果请求失败，OpenIM 返回错误对象。更多错误码说明见 错误码 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/error codes 。 "errCode": 1004, "errMsg": "RecordNotFoundError", "errDlt": ": 1004 RecordNotFoundError" | 参数名 | 类型 | 说明 | | : | : | : | | errCode | int | 错误码，具体查看全局错误码文档 | | errMsg | string | 错误简要信息 | | errDlt | errDlt | 错误详细信息 | 常见错误场景 | 错误场景 | 可能原因 | 处理方式 | | | | | | 鉴权失败 | token 缺失、过期，或不是可调用管理端接口的管理员 Token。 | 重新获取 APP 管理员 Token，并只在可信后端保存。 | | 链路追踪困难 | operationID 缺失或在大量请求中重复使用。 | 为每次请求生成独立 operationID ，并在服务端日志中保留。 | | 参数校验失败 | 请求体字段类型、必填字段或枚举值不符合接口要求。 | 对照请求体参数表检查字段类型和必填项。 |

title: 创建用户
description: OpenIM Platform API 中文参考：创建用户。说明 OpenIM REST 支持情况、接口结构和后端实现边界。
keywords: platform-api v3 chat platform-api v3 user creating-users create-a-user
content: 使用 创建用户 从可信后端调用 OpenIM REST 接口。请先在 接入准备 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/prepare to use api 中配置 API 地址和管理员 Token；接口参数通过请求头和 JSON 请求体传递。 访问 Token 与会话 Token OpenIM 管理端 REST API 使用管理员 Token 调用，客户端 SDK 登录使用用户 Token。业务后端应负责签发和保管 Token，客户端不应直接持有管理员凭证。 HTTP 请求 POST API ADDRESS /user/user register 请求示例 :::code tabs title="cURL" curl request POST "$ API ADDRESS /user/user register" \ header "Content Type: application/json; charset=utf 8" \ header "operationID: 1646445464564" \ header "token: $ ADMIN TOKEN " \ data raw ' "users": "userID": "11111112", "nickname": "yourNickname", "faceURL": "yourFaceURL" ' title="Node.js" const API ADDRESS = process.env.OPENIM API ADDRESS; const ADMIN TOKEN = process.env.OPENIM ADMIN TOKEN; const response = await fetch $ API ADDRESS /user/user register , method: 'POST', headers: 'Content Type': 'application/json; charset=utf 8', operationID: '1646445464564', token: ADMIN TOKEN , body: JSON.stringify "users": "userID": "11111112", "nickname": "yourNickname", "faceURL": "yourFaceURL" ; const result = await response.json ; if result.errCode == 0 throw new Error result.errMsg || result.errDlt || 'OpenIM request failed' ; title="Go" package main import "fmt" "io" "net/http" "os" "strings" func main payload := strings.NewReader "users": "userID": "11111112", "nickname": "yourNickname", "faceURL": "yourFaceURL" url := os.Getenv "OPENIM API ADDRESS" + "/user/user register" req, err := http.NewRequest http.MethodPost, url, payload if err = nil panic err req.Header.Set "Content Type", "application/json; charset=utf 8" req.Header.Set "operationID", "1646445464564" req.Header.Set "token", os.Getenv "OPENIM ADMIN TOKEN" resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do req if err = nil panic err defer resp.Body.Close body, err := io.ReadAll resp.Body if err = nil panic err fmt.Println string body ::: 安全提示：管理员 Token 只能保存在可信后端服务中，不能下发到客户端或写入前端代码。客户端登录应使用服务端签发的用户 Token。 请求体 下表列出请求体支持的字段。 "users": "userID": "11111112", "nickname": "yourNickname", "faceURL": "yourFaceURL" | 参数名 | 是否必填 | 类型 | 说明 | | : | : | : | | | users | 必填 | array | 用户列表 | | users.userID | 必填 | string | 用户 ID | | users.nickname | 必填 | string | 用户昵称 | | users.faceURL | 必填 | string | 用户头像URL | 响应 请求被 OpenIM 正常处理时通常返回 200 OK 。业务是否成功以响应体中的 errCode 为准； errCode === 0 表示成功，非 0 表示业务错误。 "errCode": 0, "errMsg": "", "errDlt": "" 错误 如果请求失败，OpenIM 返回错误对象。更多错误码说明见 错误码 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/error codes 。 "errCode": 1004, "errMsg": "RecordNotFoundError", "errDlt": ": 1004 RecordNotFoundError" | 参数名 | 类型 | 说明 | | : | : | : | | errCode | int | 错误码，具体查看全局错误码文档 | | errMsg | string | 错误简要信息 | | errDlt | errDlt | 错误详细信息 | 常见错误场景 | 错误场景 | 可能原因 | 处理方式 | | | | | | 鉴权失败 | token 缺失、过期，或不是可调用管理端接口的管理员 Token。 | 重新获取 APP 管理员 Token，并只在可信后端保存。 | | 链路追踪困难 | operationID 缺失或在大量请求中重复使用。 | 为每次请求生成独立 operationID ，并在服务端日志中保留。 | | 参数校验失败 | 请求体字段类型、必填字段或枚举值不符合接口要求。 | 对照请求体参数表检查字段类型和必填项。 |

title: 更新用户
description: OpenIM Platform API 中文参考：更新用户。说明 OpenIM REST 支持情况、接口结构和后端实现边界。
keywords: platform-api v3 chat platform-api v3 user managing-users update-a-user
content: 使用 更新用户 从可信后端调用 OpenIM REST 接口。请先在 接入准备 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/prepare to use api 中配置 API 地址和管理员 Token；接口参数通过请求头和 JSON 请求体传递。 HTTP 请求 POST API ADDRESS /user/update user info ex 请求示例 :::code tabs title="cURL" curl request POST "$ API ADDRESS /user/update user info ex" \ header "Content Type: application/json; charset=utf 8" \ header "operationID: 1646445464564" \ header "token: $ ADMIN TOKEN " \ data raw ' "userInfo": "userID": "2", "nickname": "alantestuid3", "faceURL": "", "ex": "123" ' title="Node.js" const API ADDRESS = process.env.OPENIM API ADDRESS; const ADMIN TOKEN = process.env.OPENIM ADMIN TOKEN; const response = await fetch $ API ADDRESS /user/update user info ex , method: 'POST', headers: 'Content Type': 'application/json; charset=utf 8', operationID: '1646445464564', token: ADMIN TOKEN , body: JSON.stringify "userInfo": "userID": "2", "nickname": "alantestuid3", "faceURL": "", "ex": "123" ; const result = await response.json ; if result.errCode == 0 throw new Error result.errMsg || result.errDlt || 'OpenIM request failed' ; title="Go" package main import "fmt" "io" "net/http" "os" "strings" func main payload := strings.NewReader "userInfo": "userID": "2", "nickname": "alantestuid3", "faceURL": "", "ex": "123" url := os.Getenv "OPENIM API ADDRESS" + "/user/update user info ex" req, err := http.NewRequest http.MethodPost, url, payload if err = nil panic err req.Header.Set "Content Type", "application/json; charset=utf 8" req.Header.Set "operationID", "1646445464564" req.Header.Set "token", os.Getenv "OPENIM ADMIN TOKEN" resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do req if err = nil panic err defer resp.Body.Close body, err := io.ReadAll resp.Body if err = nil panic err fmt.Println string body ::: 安全提示：管理员 Token 只能保存在可信后端服务中，不能下发到客户端或写入前端代码。客户端登录应使用服务端签发的用户 Token。 参数 此接口通过请求头传入链路追踪信息和鉴权凭证，通过 JSON 请求体传递业务参数。 请求头 | 请求头 | 示例值 | 是否必填 | 类型 | 说明 | | : | : | : | | | | operationID | 1646445464564 | 必填 | string | 用于全局链路追踪，建议使用时间戳，在每个请求中独立 | | token | eyJhbxxxx3Xs | 必填 | string | 管理员 token /docs/chat/platform api/v3/prepare to use api | 请求体 下表列出请求体支持的字段。 "userInfo": "userID": "2", "nickname": "alantestuid3", "faceURL": "", "ex": "123" | 参数名 | 是否必填 | 类型 | 说明 | | : | : | : | | | userInfo | 必填 | object | 用户信息 | | userInfo.userID | 必填 | string | 用户 lD | | userInfo.nickname | 选填 | string | 用户名称 | | userInfo.faceURL | 选填 | string | 用户头像 | | ex | 选填 | string | 扩展字段 | 响应 请求被 OpenIM 正常处理时通常返回 200 OK 。业务是否成功以响应体中的 errCode 为准； errCode === 0 表示成功，非 0 表示业务错误。 "errCode": 0, "errMsg": "", "errDlt": "" 错误 如果请求失败，OpenIM 返回错误对象。更多错误码说明见 错误码 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/error codes 。 "errCode": 1004, "errMsg": "RecordNotFoundError", "errDlt": ": 1004 RecordNotFoundError" | 参数名 | 类型 | 说明 | | : | : | : | | errCode | int | 错误码，具体查看全局错误码文档 | | errMsg | string | 错误简要信息 | | errDlt | errDlt | 错误详细信息 | 常见错误场景 | 错误场景 | 可能原因 | 处理方式 | | | | | | 鉴权失败 | token 缺失、过期，或不是可调用管理端接口的管理员 Token。 | 重新获取 APP 管理员 Token，并只在可信后端保存。 | | 链路追踪困难 | operationID 缺失或在大量请求中重复使用。 | 为每次请求生成独立 operationID ，并在服务端日志中保留。 | | 参数校验失败 | 请求体字段类型、必填字段或枚举值不符合接口要求。 | 对照请求体参数表检查字段类型和必填项。 |

title: 签发会话 Token
description: OpenIM Platform API 中文参考：签发会话 Token。说明 OpenIM REST 支持情况、接口结构和后端实现边界。
keywords: platform-api v3 chat platform-api v3 user managing-session-tokens issue-a-session-token
content: 使用 签发会话 Token 从可信后端调用 OpenIM REST 接口。请先在 接入准备 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/prepare to use api 中配置 API 地址和管理员 Token；接口参数通过请求头和 JSON 请求体传递。 HTTP 请求 POST API ADDRESS /auth/get user token 请求示例 :::code tabs title="cURL" curl request POST "$ API ADDRESS /auth/get user token" \ header "Content Type: application/json; charset=utf 8" \ header "operationID: 1646445464564" \ header "token: $ ADMIN TOKEN " \ data raw ' "platformID": 1, "userID": "111111" ' title="Node.js" const API ADDRESS = process.env.OPENIM API ADDRESS; const ADMIN TOKEN = process.env.OPENIM ADMIN TOKEN; const response = await fetch $ API ADDRESS /auth/get user token , method: 'POST', headers: 'Content Type': 'application/json; charset=utf 8', operationID: '1646445464564', token: ADMIN TOKEN , body: JSON.stringify "platformID": 1, "userID": "111111" ; const result = await response.json ; if result.errCode == 0 throw new Error result.errMsg || result.errDlt || 'OpenIM request failed' ; title="Go" package main import "fmt" "io" "net/http" "os" "strings" func main payload := strings.NewReader "platformID": 1, "userID": "111111" url := os.Getenv "OPENIM API ADDRESS" + "/auth/get user token" req, err := http.NewRequest http.MethodPost, url, payload if err = nil panic err req.Header.Set "Content Type", "application/json; charset=utf 8" req.Header.Set "operationID", "1646445464564" req.Header.Set "token", os.Getenv "OPENIM ADMIN TOKEN" resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do req if err = nil panic err defer resp.Body.Close body, err := io.ReadAll resp.Body if err = nil panic err fmt.Println string body ::: 安全提示：管理员 Token 只能保存在可信后端服务中，不能下发到客户端或写入前端代码。客户端登录应使用服务端签发的用户 Token。 参数 此接口通过请求头传入链路追踪信息和鉴权凭证，通过 JSON 请求体传递业务参数。 请求头 | 请求头 | 示例值 | 是否必填 | 类型 | 说明 | | : | : | : | | | | operationID | 1646445464564 | 必填 | string | 用于全局链路追踪，建议使用时间戳，在每个请求中独立 | | token | eyJhbxxxx3Xs | 必填 | string | 管理员 token ./getAdminToken | 请求体 下表列出请求体支持的字段。 "platformID": 1, "userID": "111111" | 参数名 | 是否必填 | 类型 | 说明 | | : | : | : | | | platformID | 必填 | int | 用户登录时的终端类型 https://docs.openim.io/restapi/commonFields | | userID | 必填 | string | 用户 ID | 响应 请求被 OpenIM 正常处理时通常返回 200 OK 。业务是否成功以响应体中的 errCode 为准； errCode === 0 表示成功，非 0 表示业务错误。 "errCode": 0, "errMsg": "", "errDlt": "", "data": "token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJVc2VySUQiOiJvcGVuSU1BZG1pbiIsIlBsYXRmb3JtSUQiOjEsImV4cCI6MTY5Njc1NDgwNSwibmJmIjoxNjg4OTc4NTA1LCJpYXQiOjE2ODg5Nzg4MDV9.SAu86X3PzfYjtjBeYA4vZefNr1IiFKRgg12FeiXSm14", "expireTimeSeconds": 7776000 响应属性列表 | 参数名 | 类型 | 说明 | | : | : | : | | errCode | int | 错误码，0 表示成功 | | errMsg | string | 错误简要信息，成功时为空 | | errDlt | errDlt | 错误详细信息，成功时为空 | | data | object | 通用数据对象，具体结构见下方 | | token | string | 获取到的用户 token | | expireTimeSeconds | string | token 的过期时间（单位秒） | 错误 如果请求失败，OpenIM 返回错误对象。更多错误码说明见 错误码 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/error codes 。 "errCode": 1004, "errMsg": "RecordNotFoundError", "errDlt": ": 1004 RecordNotFoundError" | 参数名 | 类型 | 说明 | | : | : | : | | errCode | int | 错误码，具体查看全局错误码文档 | | errMsg | string | 错误简要信息 | | errDlt | errDlt | 错误详细信息 | 常见错误场景 | 错误场景 | 可能原因 | 处理方式 | | | | | | 鉴权失败 | token 缺失、过期，或不是可调用管理端接口的管理员 Token。 | 重新获取 APP 管理员 Token，并只在可信后端保存。 | | 链路追踪困难 | operationID 缺失或在大量请求中重复使用。 | 为每次请求生成独立 operationID ，并在服务端日志中保留。 | | 参数校验失败 | 请求体字段类型、必填字段或枚举值不符合接口要求。 | 对照请求体参数表检查字段类型和必填项。 |
